---
title: "Structure Learning Functions"
author: "Jonah Yallop"
date: "2026-02-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### 1. Evaluation metric functions

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Function to calculate SHD, precision and recall

calculate_evaluation_metrics <- function(true_cpdag, learned_cpdag) {
  
  # Get adjacency matrices for learnt and true graphs
  amat_true <- amat(true_cpdag)
  amat_learn <- amat(learned_cpdag)

  # Generate adjacency matrices for the skeleton (e.g., undirected)
  skel_true <- ((amat_true + t(amat_true)) > 0) * 1
  skel_learn <- ((amat_learn + t(amat_learn)) > 0) * 1
  
  # Sum of cases where edge present in on graph but not in the other
  # Halved due to double count
  skeleton_shd <- sum(xor(skel_true == 1, skel_learn == 1)) / 2
  
  # Skeleton precision and recall
  TP <- sum((skel_true == 1) & (skel_learn == 1)) / 2
  FP <- sum((skel_true == 0) & (skel_learn == 1)) / 2
  FN <- sum((skel_true == 1) & (skel_learn == 0)) / 2
  precision <- ifelse((TP + FP) == 0, NA, TP / (TP + FP))
  recall <- ifelse((TP + FN) == 0, NA, TP / (TP + FN))
  
  # Calculate full SHD
  # e.g., including edge directions
  full_shd <- shd(true_cpdag, learned_cpdag)
  
  # Orientation errors relative to directed edges in true CPDAG
  # reversals, missing edges, undirected edges
  orientation_errors <- 0
  n_true_directed <- 0
  reversal_count <- 0
  ambiguous_count <- 0
  missing_count <- 0
  
  p <- ncol(amat_true)
  
  for (i in 1:p) {
    for (j in 1:p) {
      if (i == j) next
      if (amat_true[i, j] == 1 && amat_true[j, i] == 0) { # true has i -> j
        n_true_directed <- n_true_directed + 1
        learned_ij <- amat_learn[i, j]
        learned_ji <- amat_learn[j, i]
        if (!(learned_ij == 1 && learned_ji == 0)) { # learnt doesn't have i -> j
          orientation_errors <- orientation_errors + 1
          if (learned_ij == 1 && learned_ji == 1) ambiguous_count <- ambiguous_count + 1 # learnt has i -- j
          else if (learned_ij == 0 && learned_ji == 1) reversal_count <- reversal_count + 1 # learnt has i <- j
          else if (learned_ij == 0 && learned_ji == 0) missing_count <- missing_count + 1 # learnt has i and j not adjacent
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  n_true_edges <- sum(skel_true) / 2
  possible_node_count <- ncol(amat_true) * (ncol(amat_true) - 1) / 2
  
  normalized_orientation_shd <- orientation_errors /
    ifelse(n_true_directed > 0, n_true_directed, 1)
  
  return(list(
    full_shd = full_shd,
    skeleton_shd = skeleton_shd,
    orientation_shd = orientation_errors,
    n_true_directed = n_true_directed, # number of directed edges in true graph
    reversal_count = reversal_count,
    ambiguous_count = ambiguous_count,
    missing_count = missing_count,
    n_true_edges = n_true_edges, # number of edges in true graph
    normalized_by_true_edges = full_shd / ifelse(n_true_edges > 0, n_true_edges, 1),
    normalized_by_possible = full_shd / possible_node_count,
    precision = precision,
    recall = recall,
    normalized_orientation_shd = normalized_orientation_shd,
    normalized_skeleton_shd = skeleton_shd / ifelse(n_true_edges > 0, n_true_edges, 1)
  ))
}
```

### 2. PC Functions

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# PC FUNCTIONS
# -----------------------------------------------------------

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Function to run the PC algorithm once
# -----------------------------------------------------------

evaluate_pc_once <- function(true_bn, data, test = "mi", alpha = 0.05, max.sx = NULL) {
  
  # True CPDAG
  true_graph <- if (inherits(true_bn, "bn.fit")) bn.net(true_bn) else true_bn
  true_cp <- cpdag(true_graph)
  
  # PC run with timing
  t0 <- proc.time()
  pc_fit <- tryCatch({
    if (is.null(max.sx)) {
      pc.stable(x = data, test = test, alpha = alpha)
    } else {
      pc.stable(x = data, test = test, alpha = alpha, max.sx = max.sx)
    }
  }, error = function(e) { stop("PC failed: ", e$message) })
  t1 <- proc.time()
  runtime_sec <- as.numeric((t1 - t0)["elapsed"])
  
  # Calculate SHD, precision, recall, and orientation error counts
  metrics <- calculate_evaluation_metrics(true_cp, pc_fit)
  metrics$runtime_sec <- runtime_sec
  metrics$pc_fit <- pc_fit
  return(metrics)
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Function to run multiple replicates and summarise
# -----------------------------------------------------------

run_pc_replicates <- function(true_bn,
                              sample_n = 1000,
                              R = 30,
                              test = "mi",
                              alpha = 0.05,
                              max.sx = NULL,
                              seed = 123) {
  
  # Seed for reproducibility
  set.seed(seed)
  
  # Initialise results vec
  results <- vector("list", R)
  
  # Sample parameters: if true_bn is bn.fit (has CPTs), use rbn(true_bn)
  for (r in seq_len(R)) {
    # Sample from the true network with sample size n
    data_r <- rbn(true_bn, n = sample_n)
    # Run the PC algorithm on this sample and store to results vec
    results[[r]] <- evaluate_pc_once(true_bn, data_r, test = test, alpha = alpha, max.sx = max.sx)
  }
  
  # Summarise results in dataframe
  df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(results, function(x) {
    data.frame(
      full_shd = x$full_shd,
      skeleton_shd = x$skeleton_shd,
      orientation_shd = x$orientation_shd,
      normalized_by_true = x$normalized_by_true_edges,
      normalized_by_possible = x$normalized_by_possible,
      normalized_orientation = x$normalized_orientation_shd,
      normalized_skeleton_shd = x$normalized_skeleton_shd,
      precision = x$precision,
      recall = x$recall,
      runtime_sec = x$runtime_sec,
      reversal_count = x$reversal_count,
      ambiguous_count = x$ambiguous_count,
      missing_count = x$missing_count
    )
  }))
  return(list(raw = results, summary = df))
}
```

### 3. HC Functions

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# HC FUNCTIONS
# -----------------------------------------------------------

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Function to run the HC algorithm once
# -----------------------------------------------------------

evaluate_hc_once <- function(true_bn, data, score = "bic", restart = 0, perturb = 1) {
  
  # True CPDAG
  true_graph <- if (inherits(true_bn, "bn.fit")) bn.net(true_bn) else true_bn
  true_cp <- cpdag(true_graph)
  
  # HC run with timing
  t0 <- proc.time()
  hc_fit <- tryCatch({
    hc(x = data, score = score, restart = restart, perturb = perturb)
  }, error = function(e) { stop("HC failed: ", e$message) })
  t1 <- proc.time()
  runtime_sec <- as.numeric((t1 - t0)["elapsed"])
  
  hc_cp <- cpdag(hc_fit)
  
  # Calculate SHD, precision, recall, and orientation error counts
  metrics <- calculate_evaluation_metrics(true_cp, hc_cp)
  metrics$runtime_sec <- runtime_sec
  metrics$pc_fit <- hc_cp
  return(metrics)
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Function to run multiple HC replicates and summarise
# -----------------------------------------------------------

run_hc_replicates <- function(true_bn,
                              sample_n = 1000,
                              R = 30,
                              score = "bic",
                              restart = 0,
                              perturb = 1,
                              seed = 123) {
  
  # Seed for reproducibility
  set.seed(seed)
  
  # Initialise results list
  results <- vector("list", R)
  
  for (r in seq_len(R)) {
    # Sample from the true network
    data_r <- rbn(true_bn, n = sample_n)
    
    # Run HC and store results
    results[[r]] <- evaluate_hc_once(
      true_bn,
      data_r,
      score = score,
      restart = restart,
      perturb = perturb
    )
  }
  
  # Summarise results in dataframe
  df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(results, function(x) {
    data.frame(
      full_shd = x$full_shd,
      skeleton_shd = x$skeleton_shd,
      orientation_shd = x$orientation_shd,
      normalized_by_true = x$normalized_by_true_edges,
      normalized_by_possible = x$normalized_by_possible,
      normalized_orientation = x$normalized_orientation_shd,
      normalized_skeleton_shd = x$normalized_skeleton_shd,
      precision = x$precision,
      recall = x$recall,
      runtime_sec = x$runtime_sec,
      reversal_count = x$reversal_count,
      ambiguous_count = x$ambiguous_count,
      missing_count = x$missing_count
    )
  }))
  
  return(list(raw = results, summary = df))
}
```

### 4. MMHC Functions

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# MMHC FUNCTIONS
# -----------------------------------------------------------

evaluate_mmhc_once <- function(true_bn, data,
                               score = "bic",
                               test = "mi",
                               alpha = 0.05,
                               restart = 0,
                               perturb = 1) {
  
  # True CPDAG
  true_graph <- if (inherits(true_bn, "bn.fit")) bn.net(true_bn) else true_bn
  true_cp <- cpdag(true_graph)
  
  # MMHC run with timing
  t0 <- proc.time()
  mmhc_fit <- tryCatch({
    bnlearn::mmhc(
      x = data,
      restrict.args = list(
        test = test,
        alpha = alpha
      ),
      maximize.args = list(
        score = score,
        restart = restart,
        perturb = perturb
      )
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    stop("MMHC failed: ", e$message)
  })
  t1 <- proc.time()
  
  runtime_sec <- as.numeric((t1 - t0)["elapsed"])
  
  mmhc_cp <- cpdag(mmhc_fit)
  
  metrics <- calculate_evaluation_metrics(true_cp, mmhc_cp)
  metrics$runtime_sec <- runtime_sec
  metrics$pc_fit <- mmhc_cp
  
  return(metrics)
}

run_mmhc_replicates <- function(true_bn,
                                sample_n = 1000,
                                R = 30,
                                score = "bic",
                                test = "mi",
                                alpha = 0.05,
                                restart = 0,
                                perturb = 1,
                                seed = 123) {
  
  set.seed(seed)
  results <- vector("list", R)
  
  for (r in seq_len(R)) {
    
    data_r <- rbn(true_bn, n = sample_n)
    
    results[[r]] <- evaluate_mmhc_once(
      true_bn,
      data_r,
      score = score,
      test = test,
      alpha = alpha,
      restart = restart,
      perturb = perturb
    )
  }
  
  df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(results, function(x) {
    data.frame(
      full_shd = x$full_shd,
      skeleton_shd = x$skeleton_shd,
      orientation_shd = x$orientation_shd,
      normalized_by_true = x$normalized_by_true_edges,
      normalized_by_possible = x$normalized_by_possible,
      normalized_orientation = x$normalized_orientation_shd,
      normalized_skeleton_shd = x$normalized_skeleton_shd,
      precision = x$precision,
      recall = x$recall,
      runtime_sec = x$runtime_sec,
      reversal_count = x$reversal_count,
      ambiguous_count = x$ambiguous_count,
      missing_count = x$missing_count
    )
  }))
  
  return(list(raw = results, summary = df))
}
```

### 5. Edge recovery functions

```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# Function to determine how often each true edge recovered across replicates
# -----------------------------------------------------------

edge_recovery_frequency <- function(true_bn, results_list) {
  
  # results_list: output$raw from run_pc_replicates
  
  # Get true graph as CPDAG and get adjacency matrix
  true_graph <- if (inherits(true_bn, "bn.fit")) bn.net(true_bn) else true_bn
  true_cp <- cpdag(true_graph)
  true_amat <- (amat(true_cp) + t(amat(true_cp))) > 0

  # Get number of nodes and names
  nodes <- colnames(true_amat)
  p <- length(nodes)
  
  # Get count of nodes that have edges connected to them
  edge_list <- which(true_amat, arr.ind = TRUE)
  # Take only upper triangle to have each undirected edge once
  edge_list <- edge_list[edge_list[,1] < edge_list[,2], , drop = FALSE]
  edge_names <- apply(edge_list, 1, function(ix) paste0(nodes[ix[1]], "--", nodes[ix[2]]))

  freq <- numeric(length(edge_names))
  # Get frequency and proportion of which each edge was found in the learnt graph
  for (r in seq_along(results_list)) {
    pc_fit <- results_list[[r]]$pc_fit
    learned_amat <- (amat(pc_fit) + t(amat(pc_fit))) > 0
    for (i in seq_along(edge_names)) {
      ix <- edge_list[i, ]
      if (learned_amat[ix[1], ix[2]]) freq[i] <- freq[i] + 1
    }
  }
  data.frame(edge = edge_names, freq = freq, prop = freq / length(results_list), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}
```

### 6. Alpha sensitivity functions

```{r}
run_alpha_sensitivity_tests <- function(network,
                                        sample_sizes,
                                        alphas,
                                        R = 25,
                                        test = "mi",
                                        max.sx = 3,
                                        seed_offset = 200,
                                        algorithm = "PC") {
  
  # Choose replicate function based on algorithm
  replicate_fun <- switch(
    algorithm,
    PC = function(...) run_pc_replicates(..., max.sx = max.sx),
    MMHC = run_mmhc_replicates,
    stop("Unsupported algorithm: ", algorithm)
  )
  
  alpha_results <- lapply(sample_sizes, function(n) {
    lapply(alphas, function(a) {
      
      res <- replicate_fun(
        network,
        sample_n = n,
        R = R,
        test = test,
        alpha = a,
        seed = seed_offset + round(a * 100) + n
      )
      
      cbind(
        sample_size = n,
        alpha = a,
        res$summary
      )
    })
  })
  
  # Combine into one dataframe
  alpha_results_df <- do.call(
    rbind,
    lapply(alpha_results, function(x) do.call(rbind, x))
  )
  
  # Summarise
  alpha_summary <- alpha_results_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(alpha, sample_size) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      mean_skeleton_shd = mean(skeleton_shd),
      mean_orientation_shd = mean(orientation_shd),
      mean_precision = mean(precision),
      mean_recall = mean(recall),
      mean_norm_shd = mean(normalized_by_true),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  list(
    raw_results = alpha_results_df,
    summary = alpha_summary
  )
}
```

### 7. Objective function sensitivity functions

```{r}
run_objective_sensitivity_tests <- function(network,
                                            sample_sizes,
                                            scores,
                                            R = 25,
                                            restart = 0,
                                            perturb = 1,
                                            seed_offset = 500,
                                            algorithm = "MMHC") {
  
  # Choose replicate function based on algorithm
  replicate_fun <- switch(
    algorithm,
    MMHC = run_mmhc_replicates,
    HC   = run_hc_replicates,
    stop("Unsupported algorithm: ", algorithm)
  )
  
  objective_results <- lapply(sample_sizes, function(n) {
    lapply(scores, function(s) {
      
      res <- replicate_fun(
        network,
        sample_n = n,
        R = R,
        score = s,
        restart = restart,
        perturb = perturb,
        seed = seed_offset + which(scores == s)
      ) # alpha and test in MMHC set to default value of 0.05 and "mi" respectively 
      
      cbind(
        sample_size = n,
        score = s,
        res$summary
      )
    })
  })
  
  # Combine into one dataframe
  objective_results_df <- do.call(
    rbind,
    lapply(objective_results, function(x) do.call(rbind, x))
  )
  
  # Summarise
  objective_summary <- objective_results_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(score, sample_size) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      mean_skeleton_shd = mean(skeleton_shd),
      mean_orientation_shd = mean(orientation_shd),
      mean_norm_shd = mean(normalized_by_true),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  list(
    raw_results = objective_results_df,
    summary = objective_summary
  )
}
```

# Helper functions

```{r}
# Function to assign bands
assign_band <- function(x) {
  case_when(
    x == 1            ~ "100%",
    x >= 0.8          ~ "80-100%",
    x >= 0.4          ~ "40-80%",
    x > 0             ~ "0-40%",
    x == 0            ~ "0%"
  )
}
```

# Function to find the separating set - replicating the process of the PC algorithm

```{r}
find_sepset <- function(data, X, Y, max_k = 3, alpha = 0.05, test = "mi") {
  
  vars <- setdiff(names(data), c(X, Y))
  
  for (k in 0:min(max_k, length(vars))) {
    # Handle empty conditioning set separately
    if (k == 0) {
      ci <- ci.test(x = X, y = Y, data = data, test = test)
      
      if (ci$p.value > alpha) {
        return(list(
          separating_set = character(0),
          p_value = ci$p.value,
          k = 0
        ))
      }
      
    } else {
      combs <- combinations(n = length(vars), r = k, v = vars)
      for (i in 1:nrow(combs)) {
        S <- combs[i, ]
        
        ci <- ci.test(x = X, y = Y, z = S, data = data, test = test)
        
        if (ci$p.value > alpha) {
          return(list(
            separating_set = S,
            p_value = ci$p.value,
            k = k
          ))
        }
      }
    }
  }
  return(NULL)
}
```

# Runtime functions

```{r}
run_replicates <- function(run_fun, R = 30) {
  
  results <- vector("list", R)
  runtimes <- numeric(R)
  
  for (i in seq_len(R)) {
    res <- run_fun()
    results[[i]] <- res
    runtimes[i] <- res$runtime_sec
  }
  
  list(
    results = results,
    runtimes = runtimes,
    avg_runtime = mean(runtimes),
    sd_runtime = sd(runtimes)
  )
}
```
